QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_21

question.text: What is the lock-in period for ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Scheme)?

answer.text: 5 years from the date of investment
1 year from the date of purchase
No lock-in period but exit load applies for 1 year
3 years from the date of allotment of units

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ELSS has a mandatory lock-in period of 3 years from the date of allotment. It is the shortest lock-in among all Section 80C tax-saving instruments, making it attractive for tax planning. **The Trap:** PPF is 15 years, NSC is 5 years, and tax-saving FDs are 5 years. Candidates sometimes confuse ELSS's 3-year lock-in with the 5-year period of other instruments. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A and X-B cover ELSS in detail. RTAs must ensure no redemption is processed before the 3-year lock-in completion for ELSS folios. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELSS is your best tool for tax-saving discussions. The 3-year lock-in with potential equity returns makes it superior to the 5-year lock-in of tax FDs. Always highlight this to clients during tax season.
